message:
fix(raptorq): use full pivoting in systematic constraint solver

The Gaussian elimination in ConstraintMatrix previously used partial
pivoting — it only searched the current row for a nonzero entry in an
unused column. When the current row was all-zeros in unused columns,
the row was skipped entirely, potentially losing rank and producing
incorrect decode results.

Now uses full pivoting (row + column search): when the current row has
no usable pivot, remaining rows are searched and swapped in to find
one. This aligns with RFC 6330 Section 5.4's inactivation decoding
which relies on careful row/column permutations.

Also includes related cleanup:
- raptorq_conformance.rs: Rename Scenario.scenario_id -> id for
  consistency with other test structs
- raptorq_benchmark.rs: Rename mul_a/b -> mul_left/right and
  addmul_a/b -> addmul_left/right for lane clarity in dual-slice
  bit-exactness validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
